articles.title: A Big Fire Hurts a Bird Home
articles.summary: A fire burned part of a special place for birds in Kent, England. It happened at Pegwell Bay on a Sunday evening. The fire made a big scar on the land, about 300 meters long. This place is called a Site of Special Scientific Interest because it helps protect nature. Birds like warblers and reed buntings nest there in spring, and the fire happened during nesting season, so it might have hurt many birds and their nests. A person named Nik Mitchell said the damage is 'devastating' because birds were building nests. Firefighters worked hard to put out the fire and stop more damage. Now, people worry that if others walk on the burned area, the plants might not grow back. The cause of the fire is still unknown, and no one was hurt.

articles.title: Scientists Solve the Dolomite Mystery
articles.summary: For over 200 years, scientists couldn't make dolomite in a lab, but now they have! Dolomite is a mineral found in old rocks and famous places like the Dolomite mountains in Italy. Researchers from the University of Michigan and Hokkaido University in Japan figured out why it's so hard to grow. They discovered that dolomite grows slowly because its atoms, made of calcium and magnesium, often attach in the wrong order, causing defects that stop growth. In nature, water from rain or tides washes away these bad parts, letting new layers form correctly over time. This helps explain how dolomite builds up in ancient rocks. Understanding this could help scientists grow better materials for technology.

articles.title: Toads in Trouble: A Sad Story from a Reservoir
articles.summary: In Wrexham, a group called the Toad Patrol helps over 1,000 toads cross a busy road every year to reach a reservoir where they breed. This year, the water company drained the reservoir to make safety improvements over Easter weekend. The volunteers were shocked because the toads were still breeding and would have left in a few weeks. When they checked, the reservoir was silent with no toads seen, making them fear the toads died. Toads are important for nature, and their numbers have been dropping in the UK. The volunteers are upset and wish they had known earlier to try to save the toads. They help by gathering and moving toads, frogs, and newts during breeding season from February to May. This story shows how human actions can harm animals and why we need to work together to protect them.

articles.title: Blue Origin's Rocket Reuse Success and Upper Stage Problem
articles.summary: Blue Origin, a space company, launched its big rocket called New Glenn on Sunday. The first part of the rocket, called the booster, flew for the second time and landed safely on a ship in the ocean. This was a big success because reusing rockets saves money and helps launch more missions. However, the upper stage, which is the top part of the rocket, had a problem. It did not put the satellite into the right orbit. The satellite was for a company called AST SpaceMobile to help with phone signals from space. Blue Origin is checking what went wrong. The rocket flew from Florida and was part of NASA's plans to go to the Moon. This shows that space travel has both achievements and challenges.

articles.title: Singing for a Sick Lake
articles.summary: People in Ireland and the UK came together to sing for Lough Neagh, a big lake that is turning green and sick from pollution. They used songs to show they care about the lake and want to help it get better. The singers, like Una McCann who wrote a special song called 'Water is Life', believe singing can bring attention to the problem and unite the community. They say the lake is important but not valued enough, and they want leaders to make a plan to clean it up. The government agrees the green algae in the lake is a serious issue and is working on it. This event was a fun and joyful way for people to speak up for nature.

articles.title: How People Change the Earth
articles.summary: People have learned to change the world around them over time. Long ago, they used fire to cook and shape land. Today, we have big farms, cities, and trade that let us change the planet a lot. This is called the Anthropocene, which means humans are now a big force on Earth. While these changes help people live better lives, they also cause problems like climate change and pollution. But people can work together to fix these problems. By cooperating and using our skills, we can make the Earth healthier while still enjoying the good things we have created.
